data.title: Arm Holdings slides as Morgan Stanley downgrades
data.description:
Arm Holdings (ARM) shares fell 3.7% in premarket trading on Tuesday after Morgan Stanley downgraded the British semiconductor intellectual property firm to Equal-Weight from Overweight.

“Arm’s transition into chip making marks a structural evolution of its business model, aligned with the emergence of agentic AI,” Morgan Stanley analyst Lee Simpson wrote in a note to clients. “In an agentic world, GPUs (graphics processing units) generate and reason, while central processing units (CPUs) coordinate and execute. Always on, power-efficient CPUs therefore retain strategic relevance. Arm’s new AGI-oriented CPU design (specifically built for agentic AI workloads) demonstrates that the CPU is far from obsolete and reinforces the long-term rationale for deeper vertical integration (link). Arm’s talent acquisition, strategic positioning, and early design delivery have been exemplary. However, the commercial ramp will take time, and near-term risks temper enthusiasm.”

Simpson continued: “Post announcement, we think investor focus is likely to revert to Arm's in-line guide against a challenging demand backdrop. End market softness, compounded by DRAM supply constraints, could stymie near-term growth (FY27e). Margin dynamics are also in flux, with elevated R&D/engineering costs ahead of meaningful chip revenues. In addition, Arm’s entry into silicon increases the risk of channel conflict. Competing – directly or indirectly – with parts of its licensee base introduces the possibility of customer pushback, which we'd argue investors should not discount.”

In addition to the downgrade, Simpson lowered his price target on Arm to $150 from $185.

Last month, Arm introduced the Arm AGI CPU and said Meta Platforms (META) and OpenAI (OPENAI) would be its first two customers.

data.title: Industry group says health plans have cut 11% of prior authorizations
data.description:
America’s Health Insurance Plans (AHIP), the largest trade group representing the U.S. health insurers, announced that leading health plans have removed 11% of prior authorization requirements since pledging last year to streamline the cumbersome process.

Prior authorizations, also known as pre-authorizations, require healthcare providers to obtain coverage approvals from health plans before proceeding with certain non-emergency medical procedures.

In June, following discussions with the Department of Health and Human Services and the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, AHIP agreed to simplify and streamline prior authorizations, which have long added administrative hurdles and delayed patient care.

Citing a new survey conducted in partnership with the Blue Cross Blue Shield Association, the group said that findings also indicate more than a 15% reduction in prior authorizations in Medicare Advantage plans committed to the goal.

Medical procedures with defined guidelines, clear benefits in patient outcomes, and consistent utilization patterns were among the processes that will no longer require prior authorizations, AHIP noted, adding that the decision will mean 6.5M fewer prior authorizations for patients.

AHIP members, including Humana (HUM), CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Elevance Health (ELV), and Oscar Health (OSCR), signed the commitment along with UnitedHealth (UNH).

data.title: Durable goods orders fall more than expected in February
data.description:
New orders for manufactured durable goods fell 1.4% M/M in February to $315.5B, compared with the 0.2% decrease expected and the prior month's 0.5% decrease (revised from 0.0%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Tuesday.

New orders, excluding transportation, rose 0.8% M/M in February vs. +0.7% consensus and +0.3% in January (revised from +0.4%).

Excluding defense, new orders retreated 1.2%, compared to the earlier month's 0.2% gain (revised from 0.5%).

data.title: Beauty blockbuster: Estée Lauder and Puig meet for more merger talks
data.description:
The families that own Estée Lauder Companies (EL) and Puig (PUIGF) plan to negotiate the terms of the possible integration of the two beauty groups in New York this week, according to Spanish newspaper Expansion. 

The main talking points are expected to be the governance of the resulting group, which would initially be listed in the U.S., the shareholders' agreement, and the exchange ratio under a proposal that is expected to involve a mixed compensation of shares and cash. Notably, the Puig family retains 93% of the voting rights in its company, while the various branches of the Lauder family control 82% of the voting rights in their company.

The parties are also reportedly exploring alternatives with the Spanish National Securities Market Commission to maintain Puig's (PUIGF) listing, through Estée Lauder (EL), in Spain.

A merger between Estée Lauder (EL) and Puig (PUIGF)  could create one of the world’s largest luxury beauty groups, with a combined enterprise value around $40B and annual sales near $20B. The combination would bring together Estée Lauder's (EL) scale in skincare and makeup with Puig’s strength in fragrance and brand-building, including names like Carolina Herrera, Rabanne, Jean Paul Gaultier, Byredo, MAC, Charlotte Tilbury, and Le Labo. 

Shares of Estée Lauder (EL) fell 2.7% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Citi Research sees buying opportunity in Wingstop's selloff as turnaround heats up
data.description:
Weighed down by soft same-store sales ((SSS)), shares of Wingstop (WING) have lost more than 30% of their value over the last six months, a development that Citi Research believes presents a buying opportunity for investors.

The deterioration in comparable restaurant sales has caused investors to question the brand’s positioning with consumers as well as franchisees's appetite for new stores, admits Citi Research analyst Jon Tower. But the solution could lie with the company’s further integration of its Smart Kitchen technology, product innovation, and greater value messaging, making a “fairly compelling argument for an SSS inflection in the second half of 2026.”

Tower also points to healthy franchisee profitability and “best-in-class” new store returns—which reached 70% last year—supporting the idea that franchisee appetite for new stores remains high.

“While SSS remains challenged, guest conversion and brand awareness gaps have trended in the proper direction…while Smart Kitchen integrations appear to address weakening consumer scores around order accuracy and hospitality,” Tower said in his note to clients, adding that there exists an opportunity over the next several months for SSS to improve, which should boost confidence in the brand’s unit development targets and translate into a re-rating higher for shares.

Accordingly, Tower upgrades Wingstop (WING) to Buy from Neutral but with a corresponding reduction to his price target to reflect the retracement in shares.  However, the new $230 price target represents 40% upside to Monday’s closing price.

After gaining more than 8% on Monday, Wingstop (WING) shares are fractionally higher into Tuesday’s open.

data.title: BWX Technologies starts NRC process for Tennessee uranium enrichment plant
data.description:
BWX Technologies (BWXT) said Tuesday it has notified the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission of plans to apply for a uranium enrichment license for a new facility to be built adjacent to its Nuclear Fuel Services facility in Erwin, Tennessee.

The notification enables the NRC to plan resources to support its review of the license application, which BWXT (BWXT) expects to submit during Q1 2027.

The company said it is initiating the pre-application engagement process through the notification and will work closely with regulators to develop and submit a detailed license application that addresses facility design, safety systems, security programs, and environmental requirements.

The licensing effort for the Erwin facility supports BWXT's (BWXT) previously announced $1.5B contract with the U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration, which aims to help restore a fully domestic uranium enrichment capability for national defense and naval propulsion missions.

data.title: Broadcom gains after signing long-term deal with Google to develop AI chips
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) shares gained more than 3% in premarket trading on Tuesday after  it disclosed it had signed a long-term deal with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to develop its custom artificial intelligence chips, known as tensor processing units.

The deal, which runs through 2031, will be for Google’s future generations of TPUs, Broadcom said in a filing. It will also include a supply assurance agreement for Broadcom to supply networking and other components to be used in Google’s next-generation AI racks through up to 2031.

Citi analyst Atif Malik said with the announcement, there is the possibility of “upside”  to Broadcom's AI revenue target of $100B to $130B. He reiterated his Buy rating and $475 price target on Broadcom following the news.

Alphabet shares rose fractionally in premarket trading.

Separately, the two companies signed a deal with Anthropic (ANTHRO) to provide the maker of the Claude chatbot with multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity starting in 2027.

(This story has been updated with additional analyst commentary.)

data.title: Anthony Pompliano's ProCap to launch unit focused on AI-generated research reports - report
data.description:
Crypto influencer Anthony Pompliano's ProCap Financial is launching a business focused on AI-generated research reports for individual investors, the Wall Street Journal reported.

The business is expected to have AI agents that scan the markets, analyze trends, and draft reports. The agents are also expected to generate reports comparing the performance of buyback and dividend stocks, the company told the WSJ.

The agents are reportedly capable of generating hundreds of reports a day, but ProCap is set to start with circulating a few reports daily.

The reports will be called ProCap Insights, and annual subscriptions are set to cost $2,500 annually, according to the Tuesday WSJ article.

Yesterday, the New York-based company completed its acquisition of AI agent lab CFO Silvia. CFO Silvia is an AI model and agent lab exclusively focused on finance.

"Silvia is one of the leading examples of applied AI in the finance industry," CEO Pompliano had said.

Related ETFs: (AGIQ), (AIEQ), (AIPI), (ARTY), (AIYY), (CHAT), (IVES), (AIVC), (AIVL)

data.title: Blackstone opportunistic credit fund hits hard cap of $10B
data.description:
Blackstone (BX) raised over $10B for its newest opportunistic credit fund, a sign that institutional investors still see prospects for significant returns despite the turmoil in the private debt market.

The fundraising marked its largest for an opportunistic fund. The Blackstone Capital Opportunities Fund V was oversubscribed and closed at its hard cap, the company said in a  statement.

Blackstone (BX) attributes the strong demand to its experience and prior success in the space. The company's Credit & Insurance business has a 20-year track record of investing through market cycles. "Amidst a noisy backdrop for the industry, we believe this fundraise demonstrates the strength of Blackstone’s capabilities in private credit, and we’re grateful for the support from both longstanding and new investors," said Lou Salvatore, portfolio manager of the Capital Opportunities Funds, in a statement.

The fund's investments will include both performing and opportunistic ones, with the latter targeting assets that may be undervalued, Bloomberg News reported earlier.

Rob Petrini, co-portfolio manager of the Capital Opportunities Funds, added, "We believe that this is a very attractive environment to deploy flexible capital in private corporate credit as well as to provide opportunistic and structured solutions to companies in sectors with strong thematic tailwinds."

Blackstone's (BX) opportunistic credit strategy has generated a 13% net internal rate of return since its inception in 2007. The company manages $520B of total assets across corporate and real estate credit, it added.

The company's previous capital opportunities fund closed at $8.75B in January 2022, Bloomberg reported.

The $1.8T private credit market has come under scrutiny as investors worry about its exposure to the software industry, which is threatened by the rapid advances in artificial intelligence. As a result, many investors have sought to pull their money out of such funds, forcing private credit funds to strictly limit redemptions to ~5% of total shares.

data.title: Navan lands a bull rating from BofA after its post-IPO share price struggle
data.description:
Navan (NAVN) is on watch after Bank of America initiated coverage with a Buy rating on its view that the company's strong AI-driven product is taking market share from legacy travel management companies.

Analyst Nafeesa Gupta said Navan (NAVN) is an AI-driven business travel, expense, and payments platform delivering industry-leading revenue growth and early operating leverage. "Its high automation levels, strong enterprise wins, and expanding multi-product adoption are driving share gains from legacy TMCs while improving unit economics," highlighted Gupta.  BofA sees a clear path for Navan (NAVN)  to sustained strong growth, margin expansion, and free-cash-flow growth.

BofA's price objective of $17 on Navan (NAVN) is based on a 3.5X multiple of the FY2 sales + cash total. Amid Navan's (NAVN) durable revenue growth and ongoing margin expansion, the firm thinks Navan’s (NAVN) AI differentiation is translating into tangible service-cost reductions and faster booking cycles, drivers that should compound as volume increases.

Navan (VAVN) priced its IPO on October 30 at $25.00 per share, a level it never achieved in regular trading. The stock slipped 0.8% to $12.50 in early trading on Tuesday. The post-IPO low is $8.11.
